title: SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST: invoice expires_at uses chrono::Utc::now(), not subscription creation time
description: ws.rs SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST: expires_at = Utc::now() + Duration::days(duration_days). This is computed before create_invoice() is called. If the DB write is slow (e.g. under load), the stored expires_at is slightly earlier than the actual creation time. Minor drift but inconsistent: the invoice tells the user 'expires at T' but the DB stores a time computed before the insert. Bigger issue: if the relay is processing a backlog of requests, users at the end of the queue get invoices that expire sooner than users at the front. Should compute expires_at from the database clock (datetime('now', '+N days')) for consistency.
